fact_nuggets.text: Shadow Tag (Japanese: Stepping on Shadows) is an Ability introduced in Generation III. Prior to Generation V, it was the signature Ability of Wynaut and Wobbuffet.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B MD T D S Immobilizes attackers. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD Enemy Pokemon next to the Pokemon can't move! It has no effect on Ghost-type Pokemon and Pokemon with the Run Away or Shadow Tag Ability. MD RT DX Enemies next to the Pokemon can't walk. This Ability has no effect on Ghost-type Pokemon and Pokemon with the Run Away or Shadow Tag Ability.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation IV onward 1.1.2 Generation VI onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Shadow Tag prevents all adjacent opponents from fleeing (including Teleport ) or switching out while the Pokemon with this Ability is on the field. However, if a Pokemon with Shadow Tag switches in, opponents are not prevented from switching out for that turn. Using Baton Pass , U-turn , Volt Switch , Flip Turn , or Parting Shot will still cause the Pokemon to switch out. Having the Ability Run Away or holding a Smoke Ball allows the Pokemon to flee or Teleport, but not switch. If the player does not switch out a fainted Pokemon and instead attempts to flee, they will not be affected by a wild Pokemon with Shadow Tag. Shadow Tag does not affect allies. Generation IV onward [ edit | edit source ] Shadow Tag now makes the Pokemon immune to the effects of other Pokemon's Shadow Tag. Holding a Shed Shell allows the Pokemon to switch out, but not flee or Teleport. Generation VI onward [ edit | edit source ] Ghost-type Pokemon are now immune to Shadow Tag. In Pokemon X and Y , Shadow Tag will not prevent roaming Pokemon from fleeing because they will flee before the battle even starts. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Shadow Tag has no effect outside of battle.

Prevents opponents from fleeing or switching out.
